title: design: clms archaeology shadow-ledger
description: design doc + scoping decisions before any code. SCOPE LOCKED: git is universal source, mb is optional (auto-detected if .marbles/ exists). NO todoist, NO plugin tier. resolve: (a) backfill isolation - separate .claims-draft/ dir vs reserved tag vs agent-filter convention. lean toward agent-filter (one storage path, --exclude-agent already buys isolation via m-f39f). (b) confidence cap mechanism - enforce by construction (archaeology only ever emits documented or observed methods, never stat-test/code-test). (c) dedup git ↔ mb - explicit id reference only (commit msg contains m-XXXX). never fuzzy text match. (d) revert detection scope - explicit git revert + Revert: prefix only, defer semantic refute to v2. output: design.md in repo before opening implementation work.

title: core enabling: --git-sha + --created-at on add, --exclude-agent on context/timeline
description: archaeology needs to stamp historical sha + historical timestamp on backfilled claims, otherwise every entry says today. add --git-sha and --created-at flags to add (override the default git::current_sha + Utc::now). also add --exclude-agent to context, timeline, suspect so live agent context can filter out backfill entries by default. small additive change, no behavior break for existing callers.

title: implement: clms archaeology subcommand
description: walks git log --reverse and (if .marbles/marbles.csv exists) mb history. emits draft claims via existing Store::write_claim path with agent="archaeology" and session="backfill-<ts>". confidence enforced by construction: only documented (commit msg quote) or observed (sha + landed-at). detects explicit git revert / Revert: patterns and emits refutes edges. translates mb dep edges to --depends-on. dedup git ↔ mb by explicit m-XXXX reference in commit msg only - no fuzzy match. flags: --since <sha>, --dry-run (print, do not write), --no-mb (skip even if .marbles/ exists). depends on m-b126 (design) and m-f39f (--git-sha + --created-at + --exclude-agent). new module src/archaeology.rs, new Cmd::Archaeology variant. no storage layer touches.

title: docs: archaeology mode in README + schema dump
description: README: new section explaining scope (git universal + mb optional, no todoist), what archaeology can and cannot reconstruct, the survivorship-bias caveat, the documented/observed cap rule. clms schema --format ai output gets an archaeology block listing the agent stamp ("archaeology") and how to filter it out via --exclude-agent. TOP_HELP FOR AGENTS gets a one-liner.

title: archaeology v2 design (post-oracle review)
description: Doc shipped at commit 83371ac. docs/archaeology.md, 397 lines. Locks the v2 contract: pending-only commits, single drop-by-default judge, // clms-claim: annotation as primary signal. THIS MARBLE IS DONE ON CREATION.

title: archaeology v2 impl: harvester + candidate JSON schema
description:
v2.0 ships ONLY the clms-claim-annotation signal (highest-density, zero-dependency byte-walk). Other signals from the doc taxonomy (test-name-invariant, type-marked, mb-verify-task, cm-structural) are deferred to v2.1 once dogfood data shows they're needed.

Implementation:
- Replace src/archaeology.rs with v2 module
- Walk source files for literal '// clms-claim:' and '# clms-claim:' comments, read to EOL, emit candidates
- Optional adjacent '// clms-evidence:' line on next line for suggested_evidence
- Bounded-N=10 default (--max flag, ceiling 50)
- Emits candidates.json matching doc/archaeology.md schema
- Subcommand: 'clms archaeology suggest [--max N] [--output path]'
- Source filter (--source clms-claim-annotation) is no-op in v2.0 (only one source) but accepted for forward-compat
- NO commit logic (that's m-e4ce)
- NO regex crate dep, no tree-sitter, no csv crate
- ~150 LOC target

Estimated source file walk: ignore .git/, target/, .claims/, .archaeology/, node_modules/. Walk .rs/.py/.ts/.tsx/.js/.go/.md (md for completeness even though that's mostly v3 readme-assertion territory).

title: archaeology v2 impl: phase 3 commit (pending-only)
description: Implement 'clms archaeology commit --from-plan <path>'. Reads survivors.json. Adds optional archaeology_meta field to Claim model in src/models.rs (additive, non-breaking). Writes each keep:true row as state=pending claim with archaeology_meta populated and evidence=[]. Validates refusal conditions: debate=null, missing judge verdict, count(keep)>K, candidate_id collision (idempotent skip). Transcripts archive at .archaeology/<session>/<id>.json at repo root, NOT inside .claims/.

title: archaeology v2 impl: clms.judge agent file
description: Create .pi/agents/clms-judge.md per the prompt spec in docs/archaeology.md phase 2 section. Project-scoped, package=clms, runtime name clms.judge. Drop-is-default mandate, ~600 token budget for N=10, JSON survivors output. Includes 3-sentence clms-grounding preamble for fresh-context invocation.

title: archaeology v2 impl: clms purge subcommand
description: Implement 'clms archaeology purge --session <stamp> [--agent <a>]'. Reads index.db, finds claims matching agent+session, deletes claim files, regenerates index. Required for v1 spew cleanup AND aborted v2 runs. Standalone — no deps on harvester or commit work. Test: purge non-existent session = exit 0 with 'no matches' message; purge with matches = removes those claims only, leaves others intact.

title: archaeology v2 impl: v1 auto-write deprecation in main.rs
description: Remove v1 'clms archaeology' (no subcommand) auto-write path. Replace with clap subcommand structure: 'suggest' / 'commit' / 'purge'. Bare 'clms archaeology' or v1 flags (--dry-run, --no-mb, --since) error out with the JSON envelope from docs/archaeology.md flags section pointing to migration steps. Update Cmd enum in main.rs accordingly.

title: archaeology v2 impl: README + schema dump + TOP_HELP
description: Update README.md archaeology section (currently describes v1) to reflect v2 contract. Update schema_value() in src/main.rs: bump version 1.1 -> 2.0, replace archaeology block with v2 fields (phases, signal taxonomy, protocol contract reference). After this lands, schema dump should be authoritative for AI agents trying to use archaeology programmatically.
